item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s a HELL of a way to use SOAP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm stupid. I didn't notice the "Subjective" subtitle; otherwise I would have caught on (making the dichotomy clearer, and the purpose of the omniscient narrator in the first half). Anyway, good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. Literarily, the objective section takes the point of view of an omniscient narrator, which encompasses both thoughts of the characters. The shifts in perspective are intentional albeit unusual.

The “Objective” vs “Subjective” doubles as a model of diagnosis that medical professionals abide by when assessing and diagnosis patients.

Whoops supposed to be Luke. Thanks, fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adore this. I don't think I've ever felt so immersed in an article on this site, and its density makes the visceral horror elements feel so much more real.

If I have any nitpick, it's that the writing on the first page ("Objective") feels awkward at many points, with shifting perspectives and odd punctuation. And is it Paul Baker or Luke Baker? But, again, that's just a nitpick, because the story is great.

Oh, love the Foundation Medical theme too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was really quite the trip. With everything you've done, having created an article with so many moving parts, but each part being able to stand on its own, I think you have the most fleshed-out SCP on the site. You absolutely nailed the detached viscerality that makes medical horror shine, and you know just when to deviate from it so that it doesn't get stale.

Ontop of all that, you made the world's most effective HIV PSA.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recall reading one SCP or tale somewhere where a character actually said a line to the effect of "we don't care how it works, just how to contain it." I don't like that attitude, and I found the medical paper portion of this a fascinating and (for me) rewarding reversal of that usual "let's just test it until we find out how to contain, and then learn nothing".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the presentation. The idea of viewing a variety of pieces of evidence and perspectives through a researcher's emails is honestly an inspired enough framing device that I'd updoot for it alone, but then you up the ante with an actually plausible justification for a fungus inducing small-talk and some well-executed body horror. Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand the CSS theme, the offsets, the technicality of the work will be a turn off for many.

Quite the opposite for me. In fact, I was tempted to upvote it when I saw the sheer amount of work you put into this. And seconding Taffeta, the description and writing here is fantastic, and the medical documents sound real enough to scare me a bit. Good job! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP/tale/whatever is definitely really long, and the organization of the iterations could have been better.

I liked it though. I think that the extended medical documentation was very well done, and I'd like to see more like it in the future. The horror elements are really well done too. There's the neutral/cold tone aspect of Foundation writing, and it presents itself as something that could be very real. It adds a scare factor that I haven't felt for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found it oddly compelling. As someone with both a compromised immune system and a marked fondness for potatoes, I felt personally invested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand the CSS theme, the offsets, the technicality of the work will be a turn off for many.

Pretty much this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my opinion, this took way too long to go through before the SCP was actually defined. I found myself skimming in a lot of places, and I didn’t really get an emotional reaction out of it.

However, I can tell there’s a lot of effort here, and I don’t hate it, so novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't really a SCP, but it is still one of the most bizarre and stomach churning things I've read on the site. You do a wonderful job of incorporating all kinds of meaty, sloshing details into the writing here in a way that really pops.

item.title: almost feels like a rite of passage
item.content:encoded:
Commentary/Info
– hide block

My obligatory fungal/contagion and tale/SCP article, cast from an admittedly heavily and dense medical perspective, which I understand is a strength and a weakness that I own, but that I also try to ride the margins of. I understand the CSS theme, the offsets, the technicality of the work will be a turn off for many. Your viewpoints are justified and understood. I am who I am however and my very favorite works are those in pockets of severely technical niche-work, and I am accepting of the relative unconcern that comes with it.

In the hands of an author who isn’t a pathological provocateur, this would have been a normal SCP and an offset of separately-published tales.

Thanks to:
Ihp, for his crit and Listpages help, as well as Eskobar for his crit.
Croquembouche for guiding admonishing me on the css wrapper, as well as their newly released ListPages Magic and You, without which I'm sure I would have fumbled the posting of this.
The Great Hippo for use of the faded Foundation Logo on offset 1.
Tat0b910 for use of his archaeological division insignia, from which the insignia on offset 2 is derived.
djkaktus for the Darkbody Theme, that Dr Aers (thanks) then modified for his 4K contest entry.
Toa_Vine for the div code which is the only way to my limited experience that an image snippet is easily included in a blockquote, seen in his 4k contest entry's video logs (I even copied his time stamp format).
Mortos for his work on collapsibles, made aware to me via MathBrush and his 4k contest entry.
FloppyPhoenix for the Pataphysics CSS wrapper, which is here modified in hue.

If I have failed to thank you and you deserve thanks, please let me know in a PM. If you are embarrassed by my mentioning you here and wish your name to be removed from this, definitely please PM me about it.

Offsets/Fragments:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2641/offset/1
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2641/offset/2
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2641/offset/3 (<— the actual SCP article)
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2641/offset/4
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2641/offset/5

Image credits:
Magnifying Glass (creative commons)
Potato patch (creative commons)
Potato fungus (creative commons)
ACPE logo (creative commons)
Cardiovascular System of the brain (creative commons)
Potato cycle (creative commons)
